tech_lead.design_fidelity: Compared docs/tech-design/inci-attribution-highlighting.md FE-1..FE-7 against all 8 changed/new files. Both qa-lead-logged deviations independently confirmed as clearly justified in progress/inci-attribution-highlighting.md's Log, per architecture-review.md Section 6 -> downgraded to non-blocking, not BLOCKERs: (1) Gap #1 - FE-4's grep locator for 'the existing detected-active badge' returns zero hits against the real tree (verified myself); binding to ProductDetailScreen's Active Ingredients Tag list + RoutineStepCard's activeBadge instead is the only components matching spec Section 5's entry points, so this is a corrected tech-design error, not an implementation shortcut. (2) Gap #2 - AttributionTooltipProps shape verified byte-identical to the tech design's API contract ({visible, onClose, displayName, matches}); the missing fallback-copy source was resolved via the new pure src/utils/attributionLookup.ts rather than growing the props interface, which is a reasonable, minimal, disclosed resolution to a tech-design underspecification.
tech_lead.layer_separation: src/utils/attributionLookup.ts confirmed pure: `grep -n "from 'react'"` and a react-native import grep both return empty. No AsyncStorage or fetch introduced anywhere in the 8 task files (grep confirmed empty).
tech_lead.duplication_check: aliasOverrides.json ships as a raw JSON lookup typed inline in attributionLookup.ts (`Record<string, { microCopy: string }>`), rather than a named interface in rulesetTypes.ts alongside ActivesRuleset/ProcedureRuleset/SeasonRuleset. This matches the tech design's literal API contract exactly, and the shape isn't duplicated anywhere else. Non-blocking gap: rulesetIntegrity.test.ts does not cover aliasOverrides.json the way it covers the other three rulesets -- recommend a follow-up, not required for this task.
tech_lead.story_2_check: Confirmed correctly unimplemented: no bbox/image fields added to src/types/index.ts; OcrScannerSheet's new `words` field is captured but read by nobody -- ManualProductFormScreen.handleOcrResult(text: string) never references it; no 'View on label' UI exists anywhere in src/ (only referenced in test-file comments documenting it as explicitly out of scope).
notes: Non-blocking: AttributionTooltip's render function spans ~56 lines (over the 50-line guideline). New file, fully attributable to this task -- recommend extracting the per-match row into a small subcomponent as a follow-up; not required to merge.

Non-blocking: AttributionTooltip.tsx's backdrop hardcodes rgba(9, 9, 11, 0.5) instead of a token. Verified this is byte-for-byte consistent with the identical hardcoded pattern already used in BottomSheet.tsx, DeleteProductModal.tsx, RemoveStepModal.tsx, ProductActionSheet.tsx, and OcrScannerSheet.tsx -- tokens.ts has no equivalent token anywhere in the codebase today, so this is pre-existing repo convention, not a new violation introduced by this task. Recommend a follow-up tokens.ts addition (e.g. colors.scrimBackdrop) across all six call sites, out of scope for this task.

Non-blocking: GENERIC_FALLBACK_COPY is a static sentence, not literally the 'properties-derived description' Story 1 AC3 envisions. Already transparently flagged as Gap #2 by qa-lead; satisfies the hard 'never a raw error or empty state' requirement. Consider a follow-up to synthesize the fallback from the class's actives.json properties.

RoutineStepCard.tsx and ProductDetailScreen.tsx's component functions are large (~198 and ~262 lines respectively) but this pre-dates this task by a wide margin -- this diff added ~20-30 lines to each, not a material change to an already-known, pre-existing condition.
